name: BTC Trend Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow ema-touch logic
thesis: BTC 4h is the best similar-asset lane for testing whether a deeper pullback that resets positioning can still support a continuation leg once value is reclaimed. This is not shallow EMA-touch continuation; it is a broader reset-and-resume concept designed to survive mixed conditions.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset pullbacks stay too sparse, if reclaimed moves fail to continue after costs, or if the market is too range-bound for the reset-resume thesis to matter.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back materially enough to reset short-term positioning, reclaims fast value, and then resumes in the prior direction with enough follow-through for a tradable continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop-dominant conditions, some local trend bursts reset through deeper pullbacks. If the reset is broad enough, the reclaim can offer cleaner continuation than shallow touch logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is suitable because reset pullbacks are visible and structurally cleaner than on thinner assets, while still active enough to test.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Reset pullbacks may reclaim value but fail to resume strongly enough, leaving the continuation move too weak after fees.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries occur with adequate density but continuation is weak, shorten exit ambition before tightening reset depth conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A reasonable number of reset-and-resume trades with front-half exits carrying most expectancy and occasional runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after reset reclaims.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or reclaimed resets that repeatedly fail to resume.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reset depth, reclaim, and directional handoff are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broader reset logic instead of ceremonial shallow-touch setups

test_lanes.why_included: Best lane for reset-and-resume continuation in the live basket.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reset reclaims trade often enough and continue after costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Reset reclaims remain sparse or fail to resume.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Deeper pullbacks that reclaim and continue with modest second-leg travel.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset-resume idea survives on ETH.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH preserves the reset continuation shape with acceptable density.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Similar but slightly noisier reset continuations than BTC.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same broader reset grammar survives on a more volatile lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO still shows reset-and-resume behavior without degenerating into chaos.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO becomes too unstable for this continuation concept.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger reset continuation bursts than BTC.
